# Interaction Design Reference

Every interactive surface should feel intentional and responsive.

## Principles

- Make feedback immediate.
- Reveal complexity progressively.
- Teach through empty states and defaults.

## Do

- use hover, focus, and press states consistently
- keep primary actions visually distinct
- use secondary and ghost actions to create hierarchy
- design success and error states that feel deliberate
- make empty states instructive, not apologetic

## Avoid

- making every button primary
- repeating the same information in headings and body copy
- hiding the only advanced option behind unclear controls
- relying on modals when inline expansion would work better
